title: malva stenophylla
intro: Malva stenophylla, also known as the marsh mallow or hollyhock mallow, is a perennial plant species native to the Middle East and Mediterranean regions. The plant is rich in mucilage, a gel-like substance with medicinal properties, which has been used to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and protect the mucous membranes. The health benefits of Malva stenophylla include its ability to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, coughs, and sore throats, as well as its potential to boost the immune system and aid in wound healing. Malva stenophylla can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or syrup, and is also used in traditional medicine as a poultice or topical application to treat various skin conditions.
